stories.headline: Iran warns US Navy against entering Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Threat follows Donald Trump’s announcement that America would ‘guide’ stranded ships through waterway

stories.headline: Trump Announces Plan to Get Ships Out of Hormuz, Vows US Support
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US will help ships stranded in the Persian Gulf transit the Strait of Hormuz, describing it as a “humanitarian gesture” and testing his ability to restore traffic through the strategic waterway for the first time since the war with Iran began.

stories.headline: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships
stories.summary: President Trump said the US will begin Project Freedom on Monday to guide stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Iranian military warns US of attacks over Hormuz mission
stories.summary: Iran protests as Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to free stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Japan PM says Iran war oil crisis having ‘enormous impact’ in Asia Pacific
stories.summary: Sanae Takaichi makes comments during a visit to Australia, where she signs agreements on energy supplies.

stories.headline: Oil prices rise slightly despite Trump vowing to help ships leave Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil prices rose slightly in early trading on Monday as investors monitored Iran war developments. Brent crude and WTI front month contracts are trading around 1% higher at around $109 and $103 per barrel respectively.

stories.headline: Trump Says U.S. Will Help Stranded Ships Leave Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Trump offered few details on how the new program, which he called Project Freedom, would work, but said interference would be dealt with “forcefully.”

stories.headline: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
stories.summary: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

stories.headline: Trump says US to begin ‘guiding’ ships in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Detained Gaza aid flotilla activists arrive in Netherlands
stories.summary: Two activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla arrived in the Netherlands after being released from Israeli custody.

stories.headline: More than double the gas stuck in Hormuz is wasted each year, IEA says
stories.summary: Capping methane emissions would alleviate energy and climate crisis

stories.headline: Will Hormuz crisis spur Asia to boost oil reserve cooperation?
Housing crises explodes: rents up by 60% in Europe
stories.summary: A coalition of European mayors is set for a high-level meeting with the European Commission and Parliament on the housing crisis. Affordable housing isn’t anymore one of the four pillars of the European social model, warns Roberto Gualtieri, Rome’s mayor, to Euronews.

stories.headline: US Hormuz Plan Leaves Shipowners Guessing as Attacks Persist
stories.summary: A fresh plan announced by US President Donald Trump to help vessels through the Strait of Hormuz has left shipping executives perplexed, as attacks continue and traffic remains at a near standstill.

stories.headline: Oil prices flat as Trump’s plan for Strait of Hormuz fails to calm market
stories.summary: Brent crude is largely unmoved by Trump's announcement of the operation, dubbed 'Project Freedom'.

stories.headline: ASEAN states shift oil imports toward Brunei, Libya and US
GameStop launches $56 billion takeover bid for e-commerce giant eBay
stories.summary: The gaming retailer GameStop has launched a surprise $56 billion (€47.8bn) bid to acquire the online marketplace eBay, marking a potential aggressive expansion into global e-commerce.

stories.headline: Watch: The 28th EU member? Why Canada is eyeing a closer bond with Brussels
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney has arrived in Yerevan for the European Political Community Summit, where he is spearheading efforts to create a Middle Powers alliance. But could the world’s "most European" non-European country actually become the 28th EU member state?

stories.headline: Nigeria plans to repatriate nationals willing to leave South Africa after attacks
stories.summary: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

stories.headline: Taiwan president visits Eswatini days after blaming China for cancelled trip
stories.summary: It is unclear how he reached Eswatini. China described his visit as a "stowaway-style escape farce".

stories.headline: Samsung family pays off record $8bn inheritance tax bill
stories.summary: The bill is tied to the estate left by the firm's late chairman Lee Kun-hee, who died in  October 2020.

stories.headline: Hazelview Adds Presima Assets in Push to Scale REIT Business
stories.summary: Hazelview Investments Inc., a private money manager that specializes in commercial real estate, is combining its listed real estate business with that of Presima Securities ULC in a move to bulk up its global platform.

stories.headline: Hong Kong Property Shares Rally as Morgan Stanley Feeds Optimism
stories.summary: Hong Kong property shares rallied, leading gains in the broader market along with technology stocks, after Morgan Stanley boosted its forecast for home prices.

stories.headline: European Gas Futures Fluctuate With US Hormuz Plan in Focus
stories.summary: European natural gas prices fluctuated after President Donald Trump said the US will begin guiding some neutral ships trapped in the Persian Gulf out through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: AI All That Matters Even as Yields Climb: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." For up to the minute market intelligence and insight, click MLIV . (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: India Industry Hub Set for Shock Poll Results as Film Star Leads
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu, one of India’s most industrialized states, is headed for a surprise election outcome, with a film star pulling well ahead of the incumbent in the ongoing vote counting.

stories.headline: Modi’s Party Poised for Milestone Win in Key Indian State Poll
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party appears on course to secure its first-ever election victory in the Indian state of West Bengal, expanding its support across the country and bolstering his standing as the nation’s most popular leader.

stories.headline: European Stocks Fall as Carmakers Drop on Trump Tariff Threat
stories.summary: European stocks slipped in early trading, led lower by automakers after US President Donald Trump said he would raise tariffs on cars imported from the region.

stories.headline: EU Seeks US Trade Talk, But Ready to Respond, Top Official Says
stories.summary: Europe is ready to respond should US President Donald Trump follow through on his threat to raise tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union to 25%, according to Eurogroup President Kyriakos Pierrakakis.

stories.headline: Takaichi Visits Australia to Bolster Ties
stories.summary: Japan's prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, is visiting Australia to strengthen ties with one of her country's strongest allies and build on an updated regional strategy. Bloomberg's Paul Allen has the latest. (Updates to remove court footage from a previous segment.) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yen Briefly Jumps in Asia Trade, Putting Investors on High Alert
stories.summary: The yen jumped in Asia on Monday, as traders remained on edge over the potential for Japanese authorities to step back into the market after last week’s intervention to curb declines.

stories.headline: Bison Have Grazed These Lands for Centuries. Trump Wants to Evict Them.
stories.summary: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.

stories.headline: Japan sees largest protest in support of pacifist constitution as PM Takaichi pushes revisions
stories.summary: Japanese leader Sanae Takaichi has called for discussions to revise the constitution, saying it should ‘reflect the demands of the times’Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has called for “advanced discussions” on revising the pacifist constitution, as large demonstrations were held nationwide to oppose any changes to the country’s supreme law.Speaking during an official visit to Vietnam, Takaichi said the constitution, which was written by U

stories.headline: Asian currency debt gains favor as investors cut dollar exposure
Debt-financed stock investment booming as market surges in South Korea
Tokyo office rents hit 31-year high as companies move upmarket for talent
Japan to sign quantum tech agreement with India, eyeing computer exports
SoftBank aims to make data center batteries without lithium, cobalt
To Lam meets Modi, ASEAN ministerial meeting, Sony's business strategy
Japan trade show highlights quality-of-life innovations for pets
'Turn off the taps before mopping the floor': Renewables more cost effective than direct air capture
stories.summary: Direct air capture has been touted as ‘essential’ in meeting the EU’s climate goals – but is now really the time for investment?

stories.headline: Two years of DMA: Does it really work?
stories.summary: What is the Digital Markets Act (DMA)? In 2022, the EU set out to impose strict "do’s and don’ts" on tech giants. Two years after implementation, the Commission is reviewing how successful it’s been.

stories.headline: Gold Dips With Focus on US Hormuz Plan and Iran Peace Talks
stories.summary: Gold fell, following a second weekly decline, as traders monitored progress toward a US-Iran deal and President Donald Trump’s plan to start guiding some ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Merz ‘not giving up on working with Trump’ despite Iran war spat
stories.summary: German chancellor downplays US military drawbacks and president’s barbs in TV interviewThe German chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has said he will not give up on working with the US president, Donald Trump, despite a spat between the leaders over the war in Iran.“I am not giving up on working on the transatlantic relationship,” Merz told the public broadcaster ARD in an interview due to air on Sunday night. “Nor am I giving up on working with Donald

stories.headline: Iran lawmaker says Strait of Hormuz will not return to pre‑war state
stories.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz will never return to the status quo that existed before the US-Israel war on Tehran.

stories.headline: World Press Freedom Day marked in Gaza as journalist death toll rises
stories.summary: Palestinian journalists in Gaza marked World Press Freedom Day by honouring colleagues killed and targeted by Israel.

stories.headline: Trump says US will ‘help free up’ ships stuck in Hormuz Strait
stories.summary: US president says any interference with operation, which will start on Monday, will 'have to be dealt with forcefully'.

stories.headline: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel
stories.summary: Israeli court extends detention of two men who were among 175 people intercepted near Crete on ThursdaySpain’s foreign ministry has demanded the immediate release of a Spanish national it said was being “held illegally” by Israel after the interception of a Gaza-bound flotilla, hours after an Israeli court moved to extend his detention by two days.Saif Abu Keshek, who lives in Barcelona, and Thiago Ávila, from Brazil, appeared in court in Ashkelo

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill 10 as Zelensky says Ukraine hits oil tankers and terminal
stories.summary: Fatalities have been reported around Ukraine as Kyiv says Russian "shadow fleet" tankers were hit by drones.

stories.headline: European leaders converge on Armenia as Russia looks on
stories.summary: Two European summits are being held in a country long considered Russia's closest ally in the region.

stories.headline: Meme Stock GameStop Pitches $56 Billion Takeover of eBay
stories.summary: GameStop Corp. is proposing to buy eBay Inc. for about $56 billion in cash and stock, a bold attempt by Ryan Cohen to take over a storied e-commerce name several times larger.

stories.headline: Canada to be first non-European nation at EPC summit as Carney seeks allies
stories.summary: Attendance at European Political Community meeting in Yerevan seen as part of effort to build ties after US ruptureCanada is to become the first non-European country to attend a meeting of the European Political Community when the prime minister, Mark Carney, joins Monday’s summit of the 48-plus nation grouping in Yerevan, Armenia.Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US market

stories.headline: Stocks Waver as Carmakers Fall; Crude Oil Rises: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: European shares slipped, failing to build on a record rally in Asia, as carmakers declined following US President Donald Trump’s latest tariff threat. US equity futures wavered after the Wall Street gauges closed at new highs on Friday.

stories.headline: Global carmakers desperately want to be more Chinese
stories.summary: But partnering with local companies carries big risks

stories.headline: The remarkable revival of eBay
stories.summary: The internet’s flea market is back—and GameStop wants to buy it

stories.headline: Inquiry Into Fed Chair Jerome Powell Could Be Resurrected, Jeanine Pirro Says
stories.summary: Jeanine Pirro, the U.S. attorney in Washington, dropped an inquiry into Jerome Powell last month after a federal judge blocked her grand jury subpoenas.

stories.headline: Thousands in India’s Manipur mark three years since ethnic clashes began
stories.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in India’s Manipur state to mark three years since ethnic violence erupted.

stories.headline: French left-wing’s Melenchon says he will run for president in 2027
stories.summary: Melenchon says he will make fourth bid for presidency with President Macron term-limited and Le Pen facing ban.

stories.headline: Is Mali’s military government losing control?
stories.summary: A coalition of armed groups and rebels challenges the military government's authority.

stories.headline: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
stories.summary: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

stories.headline: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

stories.headline: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.headline: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
stories.summary: Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May

stories.headline: Cuba says Trump’s fresh sanctions on its economy amount to ‘collective punishment’
stories.summary: The US sanctions target people operating in broad sections of Cuban economy, including energy, defence and miningCuba’s government has said new sanctions imposed on the island by Donald Trump amounted to “collective punishment”, as an enormous 1 May procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to “defend the homeland”.In an executive order on Friday, the US president said he would impose sanctions on people involved in broad sections o

stories.headline: Trump is running out of options to contain gas price backlash
stories.summary: The rising cost of oil is becoming a major political concern for the White House, as the stalemate between President Donald Trump and Iran drags on.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
stories.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

stories.headline: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
stories.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

stories.headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%
stories.summary: The current level of tariffs charged on goods entering the US from the EU is 15%, under a deal negotiated last July.

stories.headline: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
stories.summary: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy futureLooking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: Elon Musk and Sam Altman bring their rivalry to court
stories.summary: Of the two unloved billionaires, which will the jury trust?

stories.headline: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.
